l0_summary: Calculate your break-even costs to set realistic target prices, then execute incremental forward sales strictly when those targets are hit to remove emotion and protect margins.
l1_summary:
- Calculate break-even costs to set firm target prices, selling incrementally (e.g., 10-15% at seeding and summer) when targets are hit to remove emotion.
- Store grain only if the forecasted price rally exceeds all physical, financial, and opportunity storage costs.
- Monitor local basis levels across buyers: sell aggressively when basis narrows or goes positive, and store when wide during harvest.
- Capitalize immediately on aggressive flat price offers from grain companies, as these limited-tonnage logistical premiums fill fast.
- Understand contract trade-offs: use deferred delivery to secure returns, basis contracts for local premiums, and options for flexibility while accounting for premium costs.
